title: acronychia suberosa
intro: Acronychia suberosa, also known as the prickly ash or bushy prickly ash, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Caledonia. It is a shrub or small tree with a rounded crown, producing white flowers and bearing dark green, leathery leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Acronychia suberosa include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function, and it can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve through various herbal preparations.
